blocks.content: Shirone gazed at the eye that seemed to be overlooking the world of High Gear.
Though it lacked emotion, the intent to see was clear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why an eye?
When life first emerged, the ways to sense this world must have been nearly infinite.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why choose to see?
To see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even a blind human, his eyes clearly see the abyss.
In that sense of 'seeing.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Seeing light. Seeing the world. Seeing the principles and systems within it. Seeing the heart within me.
If they hadn't chosen to see, their thought system would have changed, and with changed thoughts, their hearts would change too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In other words, an entirely different universe would be born.
Returning to the question.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why an eye?
Shirone could guess the identity of the eye beyond the veil of signals.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That is will.
The All-Seeing Eye.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The only will of the outside world to see this world, projected in the form of an eye.
Therefore, it is inevitable that living beings see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: To see what?
God.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So where is God?
Ah.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The question itself was wrong. From the moment life, humans, first opened their eyes, they were seeing God.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even with God right before them, they searched for God's existence as if they were blind.
We opened our eyes to see God.
In the moment of entering the torn veil, Shirone reached the final question.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ultimately.
What is God?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Time.
Pervading the entire universe, inescapable by anyone, independent in all dimensions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Therefore, it is an eye.
Living beings wanted to see time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A sensory organ that receives signals of light. To directly confirm the grand rhythm of this world…
That will is the beginning and end of intelligence.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yet humans search for God. Why do they think they can't see it?
Because it is too vast.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The size of time is the size of space.
Angels can confirm information from very distant places through 'overlooking.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's called time dilation.
If someone could see a planet 10 billion light-years away with their eyes…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Would time almost stop?
If someone could obtain a view that encompasses the size of the universe in one glance…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Would God be there?
At that point.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We would have seen God with our eyes, thus God exists, and we finally know about God.
Would we be able to say that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The thought ended there.
Nane.
Gathering his thoughts that had passed like a flash, Shirone exited High Gear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see what you see.
The veil closed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What, what?
As Yahweh suddenly disappeared, users watching from the ground murmured.

blocks.speaker: User
blocks.content: Where did he go? Did he jump through the code?
